page: Microsoft Azure HB176-24rs_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the HBv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features an Intel processor configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

The instance is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput inter-node communication. This networking capability is designed for tightly coupled parallel workloads. The hardware configuration lacks dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on the x86_64 Intel processor for computational tasks. No benchmark data is available to evaluate specific execution speeds or processing throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 32.0 GB per vCPU and the inclusion of 4332 GB of local storage position this instance for memory-bound and storage-heavy operations rather than general-purpose compute. The combination of RDMA networking, substantial local storage, and high memory capacity makes this server configuration appropriate for large-scale databases, memory-intensive simulations, and high-performance computing node tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure HB176-24rs_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the HBv4 family, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, alongside RDMA networking capabilities for low-latency inter-node communication. This instance does not include a GPU. Given the high memory density and substantial local storage, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and high-performance computing tasks requiring RDMA.
